stem: A two-qubit circuit applies X to qubit 0 only. What does `probabilities_dict()` of the resulting statevector return?
code:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.quantum_info import Statevector

qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.x(0)
print(Statevector.from_instruction(qc).probabilities_dict())

options.text: {'10': 1.0} — qubit 0 is the leftmost bit in the string
{'00': 1.0} — X on qubit 0 has no measurable effect
{'01': 1.0} — bitstrings are ordered q1q0, so flipping qubit 0 gives '01'

explanation.correct: Qiskit orders bitstrings as q_{n-1}…q_0, so the rightmost character is qubit 0. Flipping only qubit 0 of |00⟩ gives the bitstring '01' with probability 1.

distractors.A: This reverses the bit order. Qubit 0 is the rightmost character, so its flip shows up on the right of the string ('01'), not the left ('10').
distractors.B: X was applied to qubit 0 only; qubit 1 stays 0, so the string is '01', not '11'.
distractors.C: X flips |0⟩ to |1⟩ on qubit 0 — a very measurable change; the outcome '01' occurs with certainty.
